summary: Iteration 124: Verified screenplay tab bug fix. The expandedScreenplay state was missing from ScreenplayTab component (was only in CastingTab). Fix confirmed at line 1387 of FilmPipeline.jsx. All backend APIs pass (8/8 tests). Frontend tab navigation works without crashes. Screenplay tab renders films with screenplay text correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: BUG FIX VERIFIED: Line 1387 in FilmPipeline.jsx now has 'const [expandedScreenplay, setExpandedScreenplay] = useState({})' in ScreenplayTab
Previously expandedScreenplay was only declared in CastingTab (line 609), causing crash when ScreenplayTab tried to access expandedScreenplay[f.id]
ErrorBoundary.jsx improved: now shows actual error message via this.state.error?.message (line 30, 40-43)
TabErrorBoundary also shows error message via this.state.errorMsg (line 82-84)
seed_data_creation: TEST_Screenplay_Render film was used (created by previous iteration) - film was advanced to pre_production during testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Screenplay tab bug is FIXED. The expandedScreenplay state is now properly declared in ScreenplayTab component. All pipeline tabs (creation, proposals, casting, screenplay, pre_production, shooting) navigate without crashes. ErrorBoundary components now display actual error messages instead of generic text.

backend: GET /api/film-pipeline/screenplay - returns films in screenplay phase with valid structure
GET /api/film-pipeline/counts - returns pipeline counts for all phases
GET /api/film-pipeline/casting - returns films in casting phase
GET /api/film-pipeline/shooting - returns films in shooting phase
frontend: Screenplay tab renders without crash when films have screenplay text
expandedScreenplay state properly declared in ScreenplayTab (line 1387)
Espandi/Riduci toggle button visible and functional
All 6 pipeline tabs navigate without errors

bug_fix_details.root_cause: expandedScreenplay state was declared in CastingTab (line 609) but NOT in ScreenplayTab. When ScreenplayTab tried to render film.screenplay text with expandedScreenplay[f.id], it crashed because expandedScreenplay was undefined.
bug_fix_details.verification: Tested with TEST_Screenplay_Render film that had existing screenplay text - rendered correctly without crash
